{
  "openapi": "3.0.1",
  "info": {
    "title": "POS API",
    "description": "Cloud POS API offers services for virtual POS (point-of-sale) and data sync for front-end apps.\r\n\r\nThe API endpoints and methods are similar to those available in POS API on-premise. For 3rd party applications/integrations it will only be a technical change migrating from SOAP requests (on-premise) to authenticated REST requests. All business logic offered by methods in the REST API are identical with existing methods, and communication flow/sequence documentation will in many cases not require any changes.\r\n\r\nThe figure below shows the basic dataflow between our cloud components and on-premise servers.\r\n\r\n[Architecture overview diagram](../image/overview.png)\r\n\r\nFrontend creates carts/receipts in POS API. Receipts (POSLog.XML) are sent from the cloud to POS Server on-premise, and all existing subscribers to receipts will use existing integration as-is. Chain is master for items and prices, and all required data for Cloud POS API will be exported/synchronized from on-premise to the cloud, or requested online using relay communication with on-premises. All communication/jobs both on premises and in the cloud are handled by Integration Platform. \r\n\r\n**Provided endpoints:**\r\n-  **Audit**: Operations related to audit of shopping carts\r\n-  **CartCalculator**: Endpoint for calculating a Cart\r\n-  **Customer**: Basic CRUD of customers\r\n-  **ItemExt**: Functionality for lookup and search of items in POS\r\n-  **ItemSale**: Handles all operations related to the shopping cart\r\n-  **ItemSaleExtended**: Extended functionality for ItemSale\r\n-  **Order**: Defines contract for import of orders and related operations\r\n-  **ReasonCode**: Lookup of reason codes known by POS\r\n-  **ReceiptOnHold**: Handling of suspended carts in EG internal format. Only used internally by EG.\r\n\r\n[Authentication documentation](https://docs.egretail.cloud/ad-auth.html)\r\n\r\n<details>\r\n<summary>Cart operations</summary>\r\n## Cart operations\r\n\r\nThe following example shows the basic requests needed to create a cart for online orders, and can be used as a starting point for testing the API.\r\n\r\nThe APIKey set in NewCartWithItems, together with the store number if specified, determines which store and virtual POS the Cart is created for.\r\nEach virtual POS can have its own configuration to toggle features and behaviors and can be allocated to different countries for localization.\r\nWe currently support these languages for localized texts: Danish, German, English, Estonian, Finnish, Norwegian, Swedish.\r\n\r\nPlease note that our API enforces sequential operations on a single cart. If a request for modifying a cart is initiated while a previous modification is still in progress, the new request will be blocked until the previous one is completed. To ensure optimal performance, we strongly recommend including the 'Revision' value from the response of the previous cart modification in every subsequent request.\r\n\r\n#### Create cart\r\n1. [api/ItemSale/NewCartWithItems](/examples/NewCartWithItems.json)\r\n2. [api/ItemSale/AddItemToCart](/examples/AddItemToCart.json)\r\n3. [api/ItemSale/CheckoutCart](/examples/CheckoutCart.json)  \r\n\r\n#### Delivery and payment\r\n1. [api/ItemSale/BeginCartEditing](/examples/BeginCartEditing.json)\r\n2. [api/ItemSale/DeliverCartItem](/examples/DeliverCartItem.json), once per line to deliver\r\n3. [api/ItemSale/AddPaymentToCart2](/examples/AddPaymentToCart2.json)\r\n4. [api/ItemSale/AddGiftcardToCart](/examples/AddGiftcardToCart.json)\r\n5. [api/ItemSale/AddPaymentToCart](/examples/AddPaymentToCart.json) Multiple payment (Klarna) Note that paid amount must match delivered items or endCartEditing will fail.\r\n6. [api/ItemSale/EndCartEditing](/examples/EndCartEditing.json)\r\n\r\n#### Return\r\n1. [api/ItemSale/NewCartWithItemReturn](/examples/NewCartWithItemReturn.json)\r\n2. [api/ItemSale/AddPaymentToCart2](/examples/AddPaymentToCart2-Return.json)\r\n3. [api/ItemSale/CheckoutCart](/examples/CheckoutCart-Return.json)\r\n\r\n#### Breakage Transaction\r\n1. [api/ItemSale/NewCartForTransaction](/examples/NewCartForTransaction.json)\r\n2. [api/ItemSale/AddItemToCart](/examples/AddItemToCart.json)\r\n\r\n#### Click and collect order\r\n1. [api/ItemSale/NewCart](/examples/NewCart.json)\r\n2. [api/ItemSale/AddItemToCart](/examples/AddItemToCart.json)\r\n3. [api/ItemSale/ReserveCartInStore](/examples/ReserveCartInStore.json)\r\n\r\n#### Sales receipt\r\n1. [api/ItemSale/NewCartWithItems](/examples/NewCartWithItems-Customer.json)\r\n2. [api/ItemSale/SetSelectableCouponStatusInCart](/examples/SetSelectableCouponStatusInCart.json)\r\n3. [api/ItemSale/SetDigitalReceiptOptions](/examples/SetDigitalReceiptOptions.json)\r\n4. Payment processing should be handled via [PaymentService](https://paymentservice.egretail.cloud/index.html).\r\n5. [api/ItemSale/AddPaymentToCart2](/examples/AddPaymentToCart2-Vipps.json)\r\n</details>\r\n\r\n<details>\r\n<summary>Pub/Sub</summary>\r\n## Pub/Sub\r\n\r\nPOS API offers synchronization of data from the POS database. Choose the POS API Data Export spec in the top right to view documentation of the export format.\r\nImport into POS (currently orders) is documented here, under the Order section.\r\nFollow this [Pub/sub documentation](https://docs.egretail.cloud/Home/Doc/integration/integration) to learn how to publish data to POS API.\r\n\r\n#### Use the following blobTypes\r\nWhen exporting data to POS API, post the following blobtypes to [FileService](https://fileservice.egretail.cloud/)\r\n\r\n* __Order/Basic__: Use _BasicPosApiOrder_ as blob type. [Example of gzipped file](/examples/BasicOrder.jsonl.gz) with one order in jsonlines format. Additional orders would be added as separate lines.\r\n* __Order/Complete__: Use _CompletePosApiOrder_\r\n* __Order/Payment__: Use _OrderPayment_\r\n* __Order/Delivery__: Use _OrderDelivery_\r\n* __Order/ItemTransaction__: Use _PosApi.ItemTransaction.Import_ as blob type. [Example of gzipped file](/examples/Itemtransaction.jsonl.gz) with one order in jsonlines format. Additional transactions would be added as separate lines.\r\n\r\n\r\nRemember to use [JsonLines](http://jsonlines.org/) and [Gzip](https://www.gzip.org/)\r\n</details>\r\n\r\n<details>\r\n<summary>Migration from on-premise POS API</summary>\r\n## Migration from on-premise POS API\r\n\r\nReview this if you have an existing on-premise Soap-based POS API integration and want to migrate to the REST based POS API v1 in cloud:\r\n\r\n- When using the REST based POS API hosted in cloud, all requests must be authenticated. The type of authentication is the same for all our services and is documented here: https://docs.egretail.cloud/Home/AdAuth\r\n- Each Soap endpoint on-premise have a corresponding route in the REST API. http://posserver:8000/PosApi/ItemSale/1.0 on-premise is https://posapi.egretail-test.cloud/api/ItemSale in cloud (test environment).\r\n- Each Soap operation on-premise have the same name in the REST API and is added as the last part of the endpoint URL. The URL for NewCart in cloud (test env.) is https://posapi.egretail-test.cloud/api/ItemSale/NewCart.\r\n- The request and response bodies in the REST API are the same as in Soap, except that it is formatted as JSON instead of XML. The Swagger documentation contains examples and description of each property in both the request and response objects.\r\n- All operations in v1 of REST POS API use the http verb POST.\r\n- Successful operations return http status 200 OK and contains the same content in JSON as the Soap service returned as XML. \r\n- Failed operations that returns faults (like ArticleNotFoundFault) from the Soap service return 400 Bad Request in the REST API. The response body in this case is documented together with the response for each method in Swagger. The Type property contains the same name as the fault type in Soap. Example response body:\r\n```json\r\n{\r\n    \"Type\": \"ArticleNotFoundFault\",\r\n    \"Message\": \"The item with EAN #7024163320273 was not found.\",\r\n    \"Reference\": \"f7e0688f-b5a5-4eae-bf98-572fb2b38040\"\r\n}\r\n```\r\n</details>\r\n\r\n<details>\r\n<summary>Service instance affinity</summary>\r\n## Service instance affinity\r\n\r\nPOS API runs as multiple instances using a Load Balancer. To achieve maximum performance on your requests, it is recommended to use the same instance for operations on a given Cart.\r\n\r\nTo use the same instance for a Cart, keep the returned cookie called ARRAffinitySameSite (in a Set-Cookie header) together with your CartId, and use this cookie for every request for that specific Cart.\r\n\r\n> Note: The cookie might change for every response.\r\n> Note: If you are unable/unwilling to correlate cookie with CartId for your requests, its better to not send any cookies at all.\r\n> Note: Most HTTP SDKs will by default round-trip cookies, which is not the behavior you want for the most performance and resiliency.\r\n</details>\r\n\r\n**FAQ:**\r\n[FAQ for POS API](../posapifaq.html)",

      "ItemSale.CartIdentificator": {
        "required": [
          "id"
        ],
        "type": "object",
        "properties": {
          "id": {
            "minLength": 5,
            "type": "string",
            "description": "Unique ID of this cart, usually consists of a 4-part cart identifier"
          },
          "revision": {
            "minimum": 0,
            "type": "integer",
            "description": "The revision of the cart. It is recommended to set this in every request for performance reasons.",
            "format": "int32"
          },
          "hashBasedMessageAuthenticationCode": {
            "type": "string",
            "description": "Not in use.",
            "nullable": true,
            "deprecated": true
          }
        },
        "additionalProperties": false,
        "description": "Contains the necessary information for keeping track of the cart.\r\n\r\nIf Revision is set in the requests against ItemSale methods, the Revision number will be validated against the current version of the Cart.\r\nWhen executing ItemSale methods the current Revision number is returned in the response and this number must be used in the next request.\r\nIf the Revision does not match the Revision of the current Cart an InvalidRevisionFault will be thrown with details like the current Revision and the time for the last edit of the Cart.\r\nSee POSServices.Contracts.Common_1_0.FaultContracts.Revision.InvalidRevisionFault for more details about the fault.\r\n\r\nAll operations on a finished Cart must start with BeginCartEditing. If an InvalidRevisionFault is thrown when executing BeginCartEditing, these are \r\nthe possible reason for that:\r\n*   If the current Cart has a higher Revision number and IsRevisionFoundStarted = true in the InvalidRevisionFault, it means that another client has \r\n    started doing changes to the Cart. If for some reason you need to override this, use CancelCart and start the Cart with BeginCartEditing again.\r\n    For clients receiving Cart updates through ARTS XML, the client could also wait for the update with the new Revison number to arrive before \r\n    continuing working on the Cart.\r\n \r\n*   If the current Cart has a higher Revision number and IsRevisionFoundStarted = false in the InvalidRevisionFault, it means that another client has done \r\n    changes to the Cart. Run BeginCartEditing with the RevisionFound from the InvalidRevisionFault to continue on that version.\r\n    For clients receiving Cart updates through ARTS XML, the client could also wait for the update with the new Revision number to arrive before continuing working \r\n    on the Cart.\r\n\r\n*   If the current Cart has a lower Revision number, the client needs to correct its Revision number."
      },
